briefs.title: Council to vote on: Housing Agreement for 388-unit rental development at 6616 Telford Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a bylaw that would lock in affordable rental rules for a new 388-unit building at 6616 Telford Avenue — 304 units must be rented at below-market rates, and former tenants displaced by the redevelopment get first right of return.
briefs.action_required: If you were a tenant displaced from the prior site, contact the developer now to register your interest in returning.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to approve affordable housing agreement for 5980 Kathleen Avenue
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw that would lock in a formal agreement with Bosa Properties requiring 94 below-market rental units at 5980 Kathleen Avenue — 92 reserved for displaced tenants and 2 for income-qualifying renters.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this agreement affects rental housing supply and tenant rights near Metrotown.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on final approval of purpose-built rental townhouse rezoning in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on the final adoption of a rezoning bylaw that would allow Mosaic Avenue Developments to build a purpose-built rental townhouse project at several addresses on Bevan Street and Marine Drive in the Edmonds area.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this rezoning could affect nearby property owners, tenants, and contractors.

key_dates.label: Burnaby rezoning bylaw 14590 final adoption vote — council meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Rezoning approval for Bevan Townhomes development in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a bylaw that would rezone (change the permitted land use) a property to allow the 'Bevan Townhomes' project, a residential townhouse development designed by Ekistics Architecture.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and development activity.

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby's proposed $873M budget plan for 2026–2030
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed five-year financial plan (2026–2030) that would set the city's total 2026 budget at roughly $873M, with property taxes making up 44.8% of revenue and businesses carrying 37.1% of the property tax burden.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — approval could affect your 2026 property tax bill.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of 1030 Sperling Ave in Burnaby for 'Sperling Court' Development
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone the property at 1030 Sperling Ave in Burnaby to allow a new development called 'Sperling Court,' designed by Kennith Kim Architecture Inc. Any future building on this site would need to follow the approved development plan.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and development activity.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Vote on 10 Outstanding Zoning and Highway Bylaws
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a package of 10 outstanding bylaws from 2024, most of which would rezone (change the permitted land use rules for) specific properties to allow new residential and mixed-use developments across the city.
briefs.action_required: Check if any listed address is near your business — a rezoning could affect your area's traffic, parking, or customer base.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Zoning Amendment Bylaw No. 33 & 36 — second and third readings scheduled

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby's 2025 Bylaw Tracking Index — Key Decisions Still In Progress
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several outstanding bylaws from 2025, including zoning changes for new housing towers, updates to the Official Community Plan, and amendments affecting liquor and cannabis store rules. A number of these proposed bylaws are still 'In Progress' and have not yet received final approval.
briefs.action_required: Review the in-progress bylaws affecting your industry and attend any upcoming Council meetings to have your say.

briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a list of over 30 resolutions from 2024 that are still in progress or not yet started — covering rezoning applications, housing projects, fire safety bylaws, water metering, and more. Many items remain pending as staff work through approvals, studies, and applicant requirements.
briefs.action_required: Check the list for any rezoning or development near your business — timing depends on applicants meeting requirements.

briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is tracking dozens of resolutions from 2025 that are still in progress, including new zoning rules for small-scale housing, an e-bike share pilot, construction zone safety changes, and a 'Buy Local' procurement push in response to U.S. tariffs. Many of these items will require further Council votes or public hearings before they become final.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's 2025 Resolutions and Bylaw Progress Report
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a status update on all open 2025 resolutions and bylaws — tracking which city directives are in progress, ongoing, or cancelled. Starting in 2026, these updates will shift from quarterly to twice a year to reduce staff workload.
briefs.action_required: No action needed — this is an internal city tracking report with no direct impact on businesses.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Endorsing Burnaby Child Care Resource Group 2026 Workplan
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on endorsing the Child Care Resources Group's 2026 workplan and approving $3,000 in funding to support child care provider events and professional development activities in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you operate a child care facility — the workplan may affect approval processes.

key_dates.label: Child Care Provider Appreciation Event — Burnaby CCRG

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Anti-Racism Framework with Four Key Action Areas
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a proposed Anti-Racism Framework that would guide city-wide efforts to fight racism through leadership, staff training, community inclusion, and public reporting systems. The framework is not yet approved — it outlines goals and actions for Council to review and decide on.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — this framework could shape city programs and services that affect your business.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Anti-Palestinian Racism Framework as Reference Document
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider adopting a report by the Arab Canadian Lawyers Association that defines and describes anti-Palestinian racism (APR) — a framework meant to help institutions recognize and address discrimination against Palestinians and their supporters.
briefs.action_required: Review the report if your workplace has equity or anti-racism policies — it may influence future municipal guidelines.

briefs.title: Council to vote on adding anti-Palestinian racism to Burnaby's Anti-Racism Framework
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on officially recognizing anti-Palestinian racism by adding its definition and framework into the City's existing Anti-Racism Framework — a policy guide that shapes how the City addresses discrimination.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it may affect how your workplace anti-racism obligations are framed locally.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Anti-Racism Framework — anti-Palestinian racism recognition council vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Renewing GFL Environmental Inc. Waste Contract for 2026–2027
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a third renewal of its contract with GFL Environmental Inc. for waste services, covering February 2026 to January 2027 at an estimated cost of $240,000 — bringing the total contract value to roughly $1.08 million including tax.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this contract renewal may affect local waste service levels in Burnaby.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $240,000 contract renewal for recyclable materials hauling in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with GFL Environmental Inc. to haul recyclable materials from City facilities to an approved recycling site. The one-year renewal would run from February 1, 2026 to January 31, 2027, at an estimated cost of $240,000 including taxes.

The unit, which controls airflow in the lobby and fitness areas, has reached the end of its useful life and needs replacing to keep the facility running safely.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $5.75M contract for Burnaby fire station infrastructure upgrades
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $5.75 million contract to Holaco Construction (1997) Ltd. to upgrade fuel storage and emergency power systems at two Burnaby fire stations. The work would replace aging underground fuel tanks and old generators with modern, safer equipment while keeping both stations fully operational.

briefs.title: Council to vote on cost-sharing agreement with Burnaby School District 41 for 2026 election
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on allowing Burnaby's Chief Election Officer to sign a cost-sharing agreement with School District 41, so the City can run the School Trustee election on the district's behalf on October 17, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this is an administrative matter unlikely to affect most businesses directly.

key_dates.label: 2026 Burnaby General Local Election — voting day

briefs.title: Council to Consider BC Speculation & Vacancy Tax 2024 Technical Briefing and Upcoming Rate Increases
briefs.summary: Council will consider a technical briefing on BC's Speculation and Vacancy Tax (SVT) — a yearly tax on homes left empty or owned by non-residents. The briefing covers 2024 results and flags that SVT rates are set to rise in January 2026, with foreign owners facing a jump from 2% to 3%.
briefs.action_required: If you own a vacant or secondary property in BC, check whether the higher SVT rates apply to you starting January 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Speculation & Vacancy Tax increased rates take effect

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Mayor Requests Fairer Share of Speculation and Vacancy Tax Revenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor has written to BC's Finance Minister asking the province to send a portion of Speculation and Vacancy Tax (SVT) funds directly to municipalities like Burnaby, rather than routing all money through BC Housing. The letter also asks the province to give Burnaby the power to run its own empty-homes tax, similar to Vancouver's.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the province's response — a direct municipal empty-homes tax could affect property owners and landlords in Burnaby.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to Consider Sending Feedback on BC's Speculation and Vacancy Tax to Province
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider approving a formal response to the BC Ministry of Finance about the Speculation and Vacancy Tax (SVT) — a provincial tax on empty or underused homes. The proposed response would renew Burnaby's push to receive a direct share of SVT revenue and gain the power to create its own Empty Homes Tax.
briefs.action_required: If you own vacant or secondary property in Burnaby, note that SVT rates are rising in 2026 — review your declaration now.

key_dates.label: BC Speculation and Vacancy Tax rate increases take effect

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to vote on: $95M loan guarantee for Burnaby Housing Authority Edmonds housing project
briefs.summary: A proposed bylaw would allow the City of Burnaby to guarantee a loan of up to $95 million for the Burnaby Housing Authority to build housing near Marine Drive and Bevan Street. Council is set to vote on authorizing this financial backing, which could last up to 30 years.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this large public housing project may affect local construction and property markets.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to Guarantee $100M Loan for Newcombe Housing Development
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow the City of Burnaby to guarantee a $100-million loan for the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) to build housing at 10th and Newcombe. If the BHA can't repay the loan, the City could borrow up to $100M to cover it — with repayment spread over up to 30 years.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this large public housing commitment may affect local construction and property markets.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on $195M loan guarantee bylaws for affordable housing
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on two proposed bylaws that would let the City of Burnaby legally back — that is, guarantee repayment of — up to $195M in loans for the Burnaby Housing Authority's two new affordable housing projects, if the federal lender ever calls on those guarantees.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if you own property in Burnaby, this could affect future property tax draws.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — loan authorization bylaws vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Proposed Bike Learning Zone for Children in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider findings from a public engagement report on a proposed Bike Learning Zone — a car-free space where kids can learn to ride bikes and practice road safety. The report shows strong community support and may inform future funding and planning decisions by Council.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's decision on the Bike Learning Zone — it could affect parks, programming, or community space near your business.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Site Selection for Burnaby's New Bike Learning Zone
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a site evaluation report that ranks six locations for a new Bike Learning Zone (BLZ) — a dedicated area where children and youth can learn cycling skills. The top-scoring site is at 7679 18th St, near Edmonds Town Centre, with a score of 4.9 out of 5.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near any of the six candidate sites, monitor council's decision — it could affect nearby traffic and foot traffic.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider New Bike Learning Zone Location
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a proposed Bike Learning Zone covering roughly 850 square metres at a location identified on a city map. No decision has been made yet — Council will review the site as part of an upcoming agenda item.
briefs.action_required: Check the Burnaby Council meeting agenda for the hearing date if this zone could affect your business location.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Approving Burnaby Bike Learning Zone Location and Grant Funding
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on approving 7679 18th Street (Edmonds area) as the site for a new bike learning zone, and on directing staff to apply for outside grant funding to design and build it. Estimated construction costs are around $350,000, with the project not yet included in the City's financial plan.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — it could open grant-funded construction and programming opportunities near your business.

key_dates.label: ICBC Community Grants Program — Burnaby bike learning zone application window closes

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of 5227 Lane Street, Burnaby — Council to Consider
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a rezoning application (reference #19-53214) for the property at 5227 Lane Street. A rezoning changes what a property can legally be used for or how it can be developed, and this proposal would affect the zoning rules that apply to that site.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meeting agendas for the public hearing date on this rezoning.

briefs.title: Council to vote on converting 35 affordable rental units to market-rate at 5227 Lane Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on allowing a developer to drop affordability requirements on 35 rental units at 5227 Lane Street, converting them to full market-rate rentals. The developer says rising construction costs and financing charges make the project unviable without this change.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it signals Burnaby may ease affordable rental requirements on other stalled projects.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — 5227 Lane Street affordable rental conversion public meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby's proposed 2026–2030 Financial Plan and 2.9% property tax increase
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on adopting Burnaby's 2026–2030 Financial Plan, which would set a 2.9% property tax increase plus a 1.9% Growth Infrastructure Investment Levy for 2026 — adding roughly $61 to an average home's tax bill and $874 for an average business property.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the February 10, 2026 council vote — this will set your 2026 property tax and utility rates.

briefs.title: Council to Consider 30+ Unfinished 2024 Resolutions: Development, Safety, and Housing
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a list of over 30 unfinished resolutions from 2024, covering topics like new housing developments, fire safety bylaws, water metering, and earthquake preparedness. Many items are still in progress and will require further Council decisions before they take effect.
briefs.action_required: Track specific items affecting your business — several rezoning and bylaw decisions are still pending Council approval.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby's 2025 Open Council Resolutions Index — Key Decisions Still in Progress
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is tracking dozens of resolutions from 2025 that are still in progress, including major decisions on housing, zoning, transportation, and business-related bylaws. Several items — such as new zoning rules for small-scale housing, an e-bike share pilot, and a 'Buy Local' procurement policy — are still being acted on and could affect local business owners.
briefs.action_required: Review the in-progress resolutions — especially zoning, parking, and procurement changes — to see if your business is affected.

briefs.title: Council to consider: Burnaby's 2025 open resolutions and bylaw tracking update
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a status update on all open resolutions and bylaws from 2025 (and any leftovers from 2024). Starting in 2026, these tracking reports will be published twice a year instead of four times, to reduce staff workload while keeping the public informed.
briefs.action_required: No action needed — this is an internal tracking report with no direct impact on your business.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Approving Burnaby's 2026 Child Care Resource Group Workplan
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on endorsing the 2026 workplan for the Child Care Resources Group (CCRG) and approving $3,000 in funding to support child care provider events and professional development activities.
briefs.action_required: If you run a child care facility in Burnaby, watch for the council decision — it may affect approval processes.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Child Care Provider Appreciation Event — CCRG

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Anti-Racism Framework with Four Key Action Areas
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a proposed Anti-Racism Framework that would guide city-wide efforts to fight racism through leadership, staff training, community inclusion, and public reporting systems. The framework is not yet approved — it outlines goals and actions for Council to review and decide on.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council vote — this framework could shape city programs and hiring practices that affect your business.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Anti-Palestinian Racism Framework as Reference Document
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider adopting a report on Anti-Palestinian Racism (APR) — prepared by the Arab Canadian Lawyers Association — as a reference document to help identify and address discrimination against Palestinians in institutional and workplace settings.
briefs.action_required: Review the report and contact your Burnaby councillor before the meeting if you have concerns or support.

briefs.title: Council to vote on adding anti-Palestinian racism to Burnaby's Anti-Racism Framework
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on officially recognizing anti-Palestinian racism (APR) by adding its definition and framework into the City's existing Anti-Racism Framework — a policy document that guides how the City addresses discrimination.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it may affect how your workplace handles discrimination complaints and reporting.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Anti-Racism Framework APR inclusion — Council vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Renewing GFL Environmental Inc. Waste Contract for 2026–2027
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a third renewal of its contract with GFL Environmental Inc. for waste services, covering February 2026 to January 2027. The proposed renewal would add $240,000, bringing the total estimated contract value to approximately $1,080,500 including tax.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this contract renewal may affect local waste service levels in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: GFL Environmental 3rd contract renewal proposed start date — Burnaby

briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with GFL Environmental Inc. to haul recyclable materials from City facilities to an approved recycling site. The one-year renewal would run from February 1, 2026 to January 31, 2027, at an estimated cost of $240,000 including taxes.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if your business uses Burnaby's recycling depot or Eco-Centre services.

briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if your business works with or competes for City environmental service contracts.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $123,475 contract change for sewer water level sensors
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on approving a $123,475 contract change with ADS Environmental Technologies Inc. to supply replacement battery packs, antennas, and 12-month monitoring subscriptions for 45 sewer sensors across the city.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — monitor the January 27 council decision if you work in sewer or utility services.

briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a construction contract worth about $1.04 million (plus a $104K contingency buffer) to Holaco Construction to upgrade the meadow and Tram Barn Plaza at Burnaby Village Museum for year-round events.

The work would replace aging underground fuel tanks and generators to improve safety, environmental compliance, and reliability.

briefs.title: Council to vote on cost-sharing agreement with Burnaby School District 41 for 2026 election
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on allowing Burnaby's Chief Election Officer to sign a cost-sharing agreement with School District 41, so the City can run the School Trustee election on the district's behalf on October 17, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — no direct business impact expected, but note the election date: October 17, 2026.

key_dates.label: 2026 Burnaby General Local Election day

briefs.title: Council to Consider: BC Speculation & Vacancy Tax 2024 Technical Briefing and Upcoming Rate Increases
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to review a technical briefing on BC's Speculation and Vacancy Tax (SVT) — a yearly tax on homes left empty or owned by non-residents. The briefing covers 2024 results and flags that tax rates are proposed to rise starting January 2026, with Canadian owners moving from 0.5% to 1% and foreign owners from 2% to 3%.
briefs.action_required: If you own property in an SVT area, check whether the new 2026 rates affect what you owe and file your declaration on time.

key_dates.label: BC Speculation & Vacancy Tax increased rates take effect — Canadian owners 1%, foreign owners 3%

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Mayor urges Province to redirect Speculation and Vacancy Tax funds to municipalities
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor has written to BC's Finance Minister asking the Province to send a share of Speculation and Vacancy Tax (SVT) revenue — money collected from owners of empty or underused homes — directly to cities like Burnaby, rather than routing it all through BC Housing. The letter also asks the Province to give Burnaby the power to run its own empty-homes tax, similar to Vancouver's.
briefs.action_required: If you own vacant or underused property in Burnaby, watch for potential new local empty-homes tax rules that could affect you.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to Consider Sending Feedback on BC's Speculation and Vacancy Tax to Province
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on submitting a formal response to the BC Ministry of Finance about the Speculation and Vacancy Tax (SVT) — a provincial tax on empty or underused homes. The proposed response would again ask the Province to share SVT revenue directly with municipalities like Burnaby, and to give Burnaby the power to create its own empty homes tax.
briefs.action_required: If you own property in Burnaby, check whether the higher SVT rates starting January 2026 affect you.

key_dates.label: BC Speculation and Vacancy Tax rate increase takes effect

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to vote on: $95M loan guarantee for Burnaby Housing Authority Edmonds housing project
briefs.summary: A proposed bylaw would allow the City of Burnaby to guarantee a loan of up to $95 million for the Burnaby Housing Authority to build housing near Marine Drive and Bevan Street. Council is set to vote on authorizing this financial backing, which could last up to 30 years.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this large public housing project may affect local construction and property markets.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to Guarantee $100M Loan for Newcombe Housing Development
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow the City of Burnaby to guarantee a $100 million loan for the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) to build housing at 10th and Newcombe. If the BHA can't repay the loan, the City could be on the hook for up to $100 million.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this large public financial commitment may affect local housing supply and city finances.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to vote on $195M loan guarantee bylaws for two Burnaby affordable housing projects
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on two proposed bylaws that would let the City legally back — that is, guarantee repayment of — up to $195M in loans for the Burnaby Housing Authority's new affordable housing projects at 10th & Newcombe and Edmonds (Marine & Bevan).
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if you own property in Burnaby, this could affect future property tax draws.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — loan authorization bylaws vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Proposed Bike Learning Zone for Children in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider findings from a public engagement report on a proposed Bike Learning Zone — a car-free space where kids can learn to ride bikes and practice road safety. The report shows strong community support and may inform future funding and planning decisions by Council.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's decision on the Bike Learning Zone — it could affect parks, programming, or community space near your business.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Site Selection for Burnaby Bike Learning Zone 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a site evaluation report comparing six locations for a new Bike Learning Zone (BLZ) — a dedicated area where children and youth can practice cycling skills. The top-scoring site is 7679 18th St, near Edmonds Town Centre, with a weighted score of 4.9 out of 5.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near any of the six candidate sites, watch for the council decision — it may affect nearby traffic and land use.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider New Bike Learning Zone Location
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a proposed Bike Learning Zone covering roughly 850 square metres at a site identified on a city map dated January 2026. No decision has been made yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — check if the proposed zone affects your business location or access.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Approving Burnaby Bike Learning Zone Location and Grant Funding
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on approving 7679 18th Street (Edmonds area) as the site for a new bike learning zone, and on directing staff to apply for external grant funding to design and build it. Estimated construction costs are around $350,000, with the project not yet included in the City's financial plan.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if you operate near 7679 18th Street, this development could affect your area.

key_dates.label: ICBC Community Grants Program — Burnaby bike learning zone application window closes

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of 5227 Lane Street, Burnaby — Council to Consider
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a rezoning application for the property at 5227 Lane Street. A rezoning changes what a property can legally be used for or how it can be developed, and this proposal is still awaiting a council decision.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council meeting agendas for the vote date on rezoning file #19-53214.

briefs.title: Council to vote on converting 35 affordable rental units to market-rate at 5227 Lane Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on allowing a developer to drop affordability requirements on 35 rental units at 5227 Lane Street, converting them to full market-rate rentals. The developer says rising construction costs and financing charges have made the project unviable without this change.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect rental housing supply and market rents in Royal Oak.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — 5227 Lane Street rental conversion vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby's proposed 2026–2030 Financial Plan and 2.9% property tax increase
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on adopting its 2026–2030 Financial Plan Bylaw on February 10, 2026, which would set a 2.9% property tax increase plus a 1.9% Growth Infrastructure Investment Levy for 2026, totalling roughly $61 more per year for an average home and $874 more for an average business property.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed tax and utility rate changes — they directly affect your 2026 operating costs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan Bylaw adoption vote
Burnaby 2026 Tax Rates Bylaw adoption deadline
